title: Africa Unseen 8K | What people haven't seen!
creator: Ellis van Jason
award_or_showcase_provenance: DJI/SkyPixel 11th Annual Photo & Video Contest -- Annual Best Video, Aerial Category winner (announced 2026)
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). Verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=...&format=json), which confirmed the exact title and channel/author (Ellis van Jason, https://www.youtube.com/@EllisvanJason) for this URL, plus web search corroboration (dronexl.co, uavcoach.com reporting on the 11th Annual SkyPixel contest) that this video is the contest's Aerial Category Annual Best Video winner. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because no explicit Creative Commons marking on this specific upload was verified in this session (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field, and a full page fetch in this session returned only truncated/JS-rendered content without the runtime -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number, this field is left unset pending a real YouTube Data API call (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process) or manual playback confirmation. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments based on the video's known genre and reputation (professional 8K cinematic aerial footage, contest-winning), NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the same no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (weights_version 'default-v1', equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
